title: Reviewer workflow + cultural-review checklist + safety-review SOP
context: For M1 batch production (60+ communication symbols and 40+ wayfinding symbols), the review process must be repeatable and consistent across multiple reviewers. The reviewer workflow must document how a new symbol moves from 'drawn' through style review, cultural review, and (for the safety subset) safety expert review to 'shipped'. The cultural-review checklist operationalises the dignity and non-discrimination rules from the design system. The safety-review SOP specifies the domain-expert sign-off process and disclaimer attachment.
objective: Document and publish the end-to-end reviewer workflow, the cultural-review checklist, and the safety-review SOP so that reviewer rotation in M1 is consistent and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Reviewer workflow covers the full review path: style review (automated lint + manual check) → cultural/dignity review → comprehension-test coordination → safety expert review (safety subset) → final 'shipped' sign-off; each stage has a clear owner role and an explicit pass/fail outcome.

Cultural-review checklist operationalises the design-system do/don't rules: checks for stereotype, offense, cross-cultural legibility, disability-community norms, regional/directional variants, and whether a symbol depicting a person or body part has been reviewed by a culturally-knowledgeable reviewer.

Safety-review SOP specifies: who qualifies as a domain expert for the safety subset, what sign-off looks like (name/role recorded in provenance), the 'not certified safety signage / not medical advice' disclaimer text and how it attaches to every safety-aligned symbol.
Document is structured so that ≥2 reviewers and ≥1 cultural reviewer can be onboarded using it alone, with no oral briefing required.
Published in docs/ and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
output: docs/reviewer-workflow.md + docs/cultural-review-checklist.md + docs/safety-review-sop.md (or equivalent combined document); versioned and licensed CC-BY-4.0.
